mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is held near the effective lower bound, equity discount rates stay compressed and risk appetite improves, so the S&P 500 has positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is falsified if low policy rates do not coincide with above-baseline short-horizon equity gains.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS stays at or below 0.50% yet the S&P 500 records negative 20d returns in more than 55% of such observations, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals weakening household income and lower earnings expectations, which should pressure equities over the next month. The claim is testable by comparing forward returns after unemployment acceleration versus stable unemployment.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20d SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if average 20d return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean by at least 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: A joint improvement in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad demand strength in consumption and housing, which should lift earnings expectations and support equity prices over the following quarter. The effect requires both series to improve together, not either one alone.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term is not positive in walk-forward tests, or if joint RSAFS-HOUST expansions fail to outperform single-series expansions on 60d forward SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: An upside surprise in INDPRO indicates stronger industrial output and better cyclical momentum, which should support equity prices over the next week through improved growth expectations. The signal fails if industrial production strength is not followed by immediate market strength.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises by at least 0.4% month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if post-trigger 5d SPX returns are negative more often than positive, or if the average 5d return after the trigger is not above zero across the sample.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains too low to offset it, real policy rates fall and inflation fears rise, compressing equity multiples and pushing the S&P 500 lower over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism is specifically inflation-outpacing-policy, not inflation alone.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises by at least 0.2% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%
failure_condition: Reject if episodes meeting the trigger do not have below-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if higher CPI with sub-1% FEDFUNDS does not predict negative returns relative to other inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Stable PAYEMS growth with unchanged UNRATE indicates labor-market resilience, which supports consumption and reduces recession risk, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect should weaken if payroll gains are offset by rising unemployment.
failure_condition: Reject if 60d SPX returns after positive PAYEMS change and flat UNRATE are not higher than unconditional returns, or if the joint condition underperforms PAYEMS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST after a prior expansion signals housing demand reversal and tighter credit sensitivity, which should translate into weaker equity performance over the following month. The signal is strongest when housing weakness is visible as a clear month-over-month contraction.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by at least 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if triggered declines in HOUST are followed by positive 20d SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the average return after the trigger is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: In a manufacturing slowdown, a falling INDPRO combined with weak PAYEMS signals a broad cyclical contraction that should depress the S&P 500 over the next quarter. The mechanism fails when industrial weakness is not accompanied by labor deterioration.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls for 2 consecutive months and PAYEMS growth is below 0.1% month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if the joint downturn condition does not produce negative 60d SPX returns on average, or if INDPRO weakness alone explains the signal better than the combined condition.
